text: Code reviews are a key practice in software development where team members check each other’s code before it is added to the main project. They help catch bugs early, when problems are still small and easy to fix, saving time and effort later. Beyond finding errors, code reviews also encourage knowledge sharing—developers learn new techniques and understand different parts of the system better. This teamwork raises the overall quality of the code and builds a stronger, more consistent team. Regular reviews create a culture of collaboration and continuous improvement, making every developer more confident in their work.
